encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: Addition of placeName, seg and measure tagging

lg.l: dattДҒ dattamanorathДҒ vahuvidhДҒ sampat prajДҒbhyas sadДҒ.
vibhДҒti prДҒyeб№Үa svapatibhavane ЕӣrД«r iva rucДҒ.

sДҒ lyaб№…vrМҘddha
kulДҒyДҒбёҘ pautrД« samabhЕ«d viЕӣuddhavaб№ҒЕӣДҒyДҒбёҘ.

div.p:
yaЕӣ ЕӣrД«
jayasiб№ҒhavarmmanrМҘpater ativallabho nДҒyako yam abhimatasaб№Ғpad asti. tadДҒ yaбёҘ povВ· kluГұВ· piliбёҘ rДҒja
dvДҒranДҒmДҒbhЕ«tВ·. kintu.
Ato vibhavasaб№Ғpadam adhigatavДҒn iti.

lg.l: dikб№Јv ДҒkarб№Үya dviб№Јas te pulakitabapuб№Јo tijvaraб№Ғ yanti sarvve.

div.p:
tatra tasya povВ· kluГұВ· sudaб№ҮбёҚavДҒsa
nДҒmadheyas samabhЕ«tВ·. tadДҒpi.

Ato yasmiб№ҒЕӣ ca ЕӣrД«bhadravarmmДҒvanibhujo timahatyДҒбёҘ kб№ЈamДҒyДҒs sudДҒб№…kumuvelВ·kб№Јe
trДҒб№Үi yo dhigatavДҒn iti. Api ca.

lg.l: kД«rttir vvibhramatД«va dikб№Јu vitatДҒ nirggantum asmДҒt paraб№Ғ.

prД«
tyДҒ samДҒkarб№Үб№Үya nrМҘpДҒЕӣ ca tuб№Јб№ӯДҒs

div.p:
tadrДҒjye yas tu prДҒyaЕӣo bhЕ«pater ДҒjГұДҒsupra
yatnakrМҘtДҒyДҒsatvДҒd akДҒlДҒdhipatinДҒmadheyam avДҒpa.

Asti ya
dagrajas sukrМҘtД« povВ· kluГұВ· dharmmapДҒб№ӯhДҒkhyaбёҘ ЕӣrД« IndravarmmДҒvanipДҒlДҒnugraho bhЕ«tВ·.

yenДҒyam agrajo iДҒm evam akathayatВ·. kumuvelВ· bhЕ«mikб№ЈetrДҒб№Үi rДҒjaprasДҒdДҒd adhigatДҒ
ny abhЕ«vanВ·. cikirgrДҒmo cДҒtmajananД«nivДҒsabhЕ«mi sthДҒnayoбёҘ devaбёҘ pratiб№Јб№ӯhДҒpayitavya I
ti. kutaбёҘ.

sa Eva paramakuЕӣalapuб№Үyasaб№ҒbhДҒrabhДҒrДҒva
rjjitahrМҘdayo mvaradahanamaб№…galopagate ЕӣakarДҒje mithunДҒdityaketvindutanaye mrМҘgendra
lagne mrМҘgarДҒjДҒkhaб№ҮбёҚatuб№ЈДҒrДҒб№ҒЕӣau kanyДҒbhЕ«tanaye makararДҒhau meб№Јasuragururavije magharkб№Јe
somavДҒre jeyeб№Јб№ӯhasitapaГұcДҒhe devaliб№…geЕӣvaram atiб№Јб№ӯhipat svДҒgrajena saha kД«rttaya
Iti.

niyВ· doб№Ғ humДҒ yДҒб№Ғб№…В· povВ· ku devaliб№…geЕӣvara di kumuvvelВ·. humДҒ bhaukВ· puДҖkВ· tluvВ· pluбёҘ tijuбёҘ galaukВ·. A
siyВ· ГұЕ« sa pluбёҘ jДҒkВ·. nariyВ· sДҒб№…В· UrДҒб№…В· pagДҒrВ· pinДҒб№…В· tЕ«yВ· AndapВ· sdДҒб№…В· nau pataб№ҒlВ· dandau viЕӣeб№Јa. sumrДҒб№…В·
dakб№Јiб№Үa naб№Ғ pluбёҘ galauk. AsiyВ· ГұЕ« tluvВ· pluбёҘ jДҒkВ·. nariyВ· simВ· pataб№ҒlВ· dvДҒ sumrДҒб№…В· dandau viЕӣeб№Јa pataб№ҒlВ· hu
mДҒ krainВ· б№…anВ· humДҒ talavvДҒrВ· tluvВ· rtuбёҘ sДҒ pluбёҘ galaukВ·. AsiyВ· ГұЕ« dalapanВ· pluбёҘ jДҒkВ·. niyВ· humДҒ daniбёҘ trДҒ
nariyВ· simВ· pataб№ҒlВ· sДҒб№…В· povВ· AcovВ· vuб№…ДҒ. tijuбёҘ pluбёҘ tluvВ· galaukВ·. nariyВ· dandau viЕӣeб№Јa pataб№ҒlВ· rumaбёҘ yДҒб№…В· sДҒ
rtuбёҘ naб№Ғ pluбёҘ dvДҒ galaukВ·. humДҒ vrajaб№Ғб№…В· AviбёҘ lagaiбёҘ di yДҒб№…В·. nariyВ· likukВ· dlai rumaбёҘ yДҒб№…В· pataб№ҒlВ· dlai vandДҒб№…В· ru
maб№…В· dlai vandДҒб№…В· pataб№ҒlВ· dlai padanВ· nariyВ· AjauГұВ· dlai nanВ· nau bhaukВ· huriyВ· tamДҒ AviбёҘ nau. nariyВ· dlai Ajau
ГұВ· curaiбёҘ pataб№ҒlВ· AjauГұВ· dlai dandau rivuvВ·. dlai pulДҒvВ· dandau rivuvВ·. tumВ· jalДҒnВ· virДҒnВ· niyВ· dandau trДҒ. dandau
rivuvВ·. dandau rusДҒ. dandau tlanВ·. dandau AjauГұВ· curaiбёҘ . dandau krainВ·. dandau AkyakВ·. dandau kmaб№ҒnВ·. dandau kuГұitВ·.
dandau viЕӣeб№Јa. tumВ· nariyВ· danau rivuvВ· pataб№ҒlВ· stapВ· Uttara hajai yДҒб№…В· povВ· ku trivikramapura. rumaб№…В· da
kб№Јiб№Үa hajai yДҒб№…В· povВ· ku trivikramapura pataб№ҒlВ· stapВ· harВ· tanipДҒrВ· UrДҒб№…В· cvaбёҘ nanВ· б№…anВ· tumВ· nanВ· A
vista lagaiбёҘ di yДҒб№…В· povВ· ku siniyВ·.*

app.note: Note clear absence of anusvДҒra after yavadvД«pa in st. XI. Read yavadvД«papuraб№Ғ.
Our reading was proposed by Yuko Yokochi.
Huber did the wrong scansion of the first syllable.
Perhaps restore nijakathДҒm, although the shape of the ДҒ-vocalisation rather makes one suspect its supporting consonant to be б№…, j, Гұ or б№ӯ.
Huber identifies this passages as an ДҖryДҒ stanza (nr. XVII), but this cannot be correct. We believe the passage is prose.
Huber admitted that the reading was uncertain. The combination pu ДҖk also occurs in C. 148, l. C. 3. Another combination, pu AUkВ·, occurs in C. 30B1, l. 4-5 (should it be corrected in pu ДҖk?)

div.p: Homage be to him whom the people of the past, present and future in the three worlds BhЕ«, Bhuvas, Svas, praised, praise and will praise.

The one whose valor is equal to that of ЕҡrД«вҖҷs husband Viб№Јб№Үu; who fearlessly protects the land of CampДҒ, like Lakб№ЈmД« unwavering despite her own velocity being intact; who always gives to his subjects manifold wealth to fulfill their desires: he is called on earth the illustrious king ЕҡrД« Jayasiб№Ғhavarman.

In his royal palaces, the virtuous satД«, incomparable ЕҡrД« TribhuvanamahДҒdevД«, the chief queen among royal consorts, the foremost of all, devoted to virtuous deeds, is resplendent with her offspring almost like ЕҡrД« with her beauty in her husbandвҖҷs palace.
She was the grand-daughter of Lyaб№… VrМҘddhakulДҒ, whose lineages were pure, equal as she was to the full moon, and illuminator of the sky which were her two lineages.

Furthermore the virtuous first-born of Lyaб№… VrМҘddhakulДҒ, NarendrДҒdhipati, who offered devotion to the lotus-feet of the king, whose nature was more desirable than Nature itself, had a distinguished daughter, whose own lineage was thoroughly purified by her nature, and she had a son of renowned conduct whose intentions were well-disposed to service of the feet of kings.
Wise, endowed with self-control, virtuous and of skilful comportment, he had his whole body inclined to a kingвҖҷs command, out of his infinite devotion.
He is this captain, most favorite of the king ЕҡrД« Jayasiб№Ғhavarman, if enviable riches, who was then named Pov KluГұ PiliбёҘ RДҒjadvДҒra. But:
Having been assigned by the king with a diplomatic task, this dignitary went to the capital of Yava-Island, and met with success of his mission.
For this reason, he obtained the fortune of an exalted position.

Subsequently, he who by the radiant order of king ЕҡrД« JayaЕӣakti, the son of king ЕҡrД« Jayasiб№Ғhavarman, who protected the earth, was similarly eminent prabhЕ«ta during his illustrious reign вҖ” filled everywhere with the production of all manner of wealth.
Furthermore:
Having heard of the illustrious king ЕҡrД« Bhadravarman here , after having taken in battle the of the enemies, those foes enter a very feverish state, their appearances bristling with excitement
On that occasion, he obtained the name Pov KluГұ Sudaб№ҮбёҚavДҒsa. And then:
By this same assignment of the king he again went to the capital of YavadvД«pa, and even the second time obtained success of his mission.

It is as if the fame of him, the son of the king called ЕҡrД« Bhadravarman, who protects the entire earth that is once again very stable, and has an abundance of fortune thanks to hard-won victories, while she his fame is wandering around and has become spread out in all directions in order to get away from him, spoke, вҖҳMay this woman laughing at me, Universal Direction, become his (the kingвҖҷs) increasing battle-prize.вҖҷ
After having heard about that lord ЕҡrД« Indravarman, the branches of whose fame had spread even in a foreign land, the happy kings praised him uninterruptedly.
Thanks to the fact that in general he was dedicated and zealous with regard to the kingвҖҷs order during his reign , he got the name AkДҒlДҒdhipati.

Prosperous in nature due to actions resulting from good qualities, his intelligence intent upon the policies of the king, he was evidently able somewhat to give some instructions in what is desirable and undesirable for the king.
He had a virtuous first-born. Being one whose state of grace was due to king ЕҡrД« Indravarman, he became named Pov KluГұ DharmapДҒб№ӯha.
вҖҳHere, beyond the ocean of virtues, he was the Sunrise-mountain of mind, pure like crystal that is brilliant by nature, being a store-house of the riches which are clever thoughts'

For which reason yena this firstborn son spoke in this way: 'The fields in the land of Kumuvel had been obtained by grace of the king. And in the village of Cikir, both at the land where his own mother resides and at the place sthДҒna, God is to be established. Why?

This wealth is in every true scripture described by the Great Sage the Buddha as meaningless, even heaped up, even in great quantities, as perishable in an instant, as altogether the least substantial thing on earth.вҖҷ
Having had this thought, keen on glory in this world, the intelligent one made efforts to carry out the foundation of the god at the two places.
He, who heart was inclined by the weight of the equipment of merit thanks to the highest virtue, when the Еҡaka king was marked by Space 0, Fires 3, Auspicious Marks
None.

Just as the earth dharДҒ, though fickle, having resorted to the mountain/king, obtains stability, so does this country come to stability after taking recourse either to MaheЕӣvara or also to the Compassionate one LokeЕӣvara, due to their power.

These are all of the fields of Y.P.K. Devaliб№…geЕӣvara at Kumuvvel - the field Bhauk Pu Ak: thirty-seven galauk, its product ten jДҒk, from the houses of the PagДҒr PinДҒб№… people, following the front andap of SdДҒб№…, going up to the reserved viЕӣeб№Јa tank, on the southern bank: sixty galauk, its product thirty jДҒk, from the boundary marker up to the second point; on the southern bank of the reserved tank up to the field of Krain and the field of TalavДҒr: three hundred and ten galauk, its product eighty jДҒk.

And these are the sowing fields - from the boundary marker up to the house of Master Flower Chief: seventy-three galauk; from the reserved tank up to the godвҖҷs abode: one hundred and sixty-two galauk;

All of the Vrajaб№… fields dear to God - from the back of the forest of the godвҖҷs abode to the open vandДҒб№… forest of: forest of VandДҒб№…; from the open forest to the Pandan forest; from the ajauГұ of that forest to the hill bhauk of the sunset huriy tamДҒ, everything goes; from the forest of ajauГұ lotuses to the ajauГұ of the forest of a thousand tanks, the forest of the island of a thousand tanks; the stream tum of this northern road and the tanks: the thousand tanks; the deer tank; the python tank; the tank of ajauГұ lotuses; the krain tank; the brick ? akyak tank; the kmaб№Ғn tank; the curcuma tank; the reserved tank; the stream from the thousand tanks to the northern stap of the domain of Y.P.K. of Trivikramapura; from the south of the domain of Y.P.K. of Trivikramapura to the stap of the torrent which serves as crossing for the people of the district; and all the streams dear to the Y.P.K. of this place.
Adoration Г Еҡiva ! Bonheur !

p.supplied: Stance mutilГ©e en l'honneur de Еҡiva.

div.p: Il y avait un roi, valeureux comme l'Г©poux de Lakб№ЈmД« Viб№Јб№Үu lui-mГӘme ; il gouvernait avec Г©nergie la terre du CampДҒ qui Г©tait dans la plГ©nitude de sa force et qui lui Г©tait fidГЁle comme Lakб№ЈmД« l'est Г son Г©poux ; sans cesse il procurait Г ses sujets un bonheur multiple qui laissait plus de place au dГ©sir ; ce roi s'appelait sur la terre S. M. ЕҡrД«-Jayasiб№Ғhavarman.

Celle qui fut sa reine principale, ЕҡrД«-TribhuvanadevД«, sans Г©gale parmi les femmes des rois, elle qui est la premiГЁre au palais royal, cette haute date d'une conduite vertueuse, brille, mГӘme aprГЁs la mort du roi, d'un Г©clat comparable Г celui de l'Г©pouse de Viб№Јб№Үu ; combien plus encore du vivant de son royal Г©poux !
Elle est la petite-fille de la Lyaб№… VrМҘddhakulДҒ, de cette femme issue d'une race pure qui, telle une pleine lune, a illuminГ© le ciel de deux races. Ensuite :
Cette Lyaб№… VrМҘddhakulДҒ avait eu un fils aГ®nГ©, le chef-d'Е“uvre de la nature, dГ©vouГ© au service des lotus des pieds royaux de son souverain.

De celui-ci naquit une fille et d'elle qui pouvait se vanter d'appartenir Г une famille pure dГЁs l'origine, naquit un fils ; d'une conduite exemplaire il est dГ©vouГ© uniquement au service de son roi.
Rempli de bon sens, discret, pieux, bienfaisant, cet homme loyal ne vit que pour les ordres de son souverain.
Il eut le bonheur enviable d'ГӘtre un des familiers et un des capitaines de S. M. ЕҡrД«-Jayasiб№Ғhavarman ; il s'appelait Po KluГұ PiliбёҘ RДҒjadvДҒra.
Pour un but louable, lui qui est ferme de propos, il se rendit, avec l'autorisation du roi, dans la capitale de Java oГ№ il acquit la science magique.
De lГ date sa grande fortune.
Sous le rГЁgne brillant et parfaitement prospГЁre de S. M. ЕҡrД«-JayaЕӣaktivarman, fils de S. M. Jayasiб№Ғhavarman.... il Po KluГұ PiliбёҘ RДҒjadvДҒra continuait Г occuper une haute situation.
Stance mutilГ©e : Гүloge du roi ЕҡrД«-Bhadravarman, qui Г©tait terrible Г ses ennemis.
Sous ce rГЁgne il Po KluГұ PiliбёҘ RДҒjadvДҒra reГ§ut le titre de Po KluГұ Sudaб№ҮбёҚavДҒsa.
.... avec la permission du roi il se rendit une seconde fois Г Java pour acquГ©rir la science magique.
.... il obtint de S. M. ЕҡrД« Bhadravarman.. les terres des villages de SudДҒб№… et de Kumuvel.
Stance mutilГ©e : Ensuite rГ©gnait le fils de SrД«-Bhadravarmman, ЕҡrД« Indravarman ; dГ©but de son Г©loge.
Les rois, heureux et l'obГ©issant avec dГ©votion, ne cessent de changer les louanges de S. M. ЕҡrД«-Indravarman, dont la gloire magnifique est rГ©pandue jusqu'aux confins de la terre.
Sous son rГЁgne il PiliбёҘ RДҒjadvДҒra a obtenu le titre de AkДҒlДҒdhipati, en rГ©compense du zГЁle avec lequel il accomplit les ordres du roi.
..... mettant toute son intelligence au service de la politique du roi, il est Г mГӘme de... conseiller le souverain dans les circonstances heureuses et malheureuses.
Son fils aГ®nГ© s'appelle SukrМҘtД« Po KluГұ DharmapДҒtha ; c'est un des favoris de S. M. ЕҡrД«-Indravarman.
Stance mutilГ©e : Г©loge de Po KluГұ DharmapДҒtha, le fils aГ®nГ© du donateur.

.... il parla ainsi Г son fils aГ®nГ© : La faveur du roi nous a valu les terres du village de Kumuvel ; le village de Cikir, oГ№ nous sommes nГ©s et oГ№ nous demeurons............... ....... il convient que nous deux, d'un cЕ“ur...... nous Г©rigions un temple. Pourquoi ?

"Les biens de ce monde, tant que vous en aurez, n'ont aucune consistence ; amassez-en tant que vous voudrez : un seul instant vous les ravira ; rien n'est aussi futile sur cette terre." VoilГ ce que le Grand Sage le Buddha n'a cessГ© de proclamer dans son excellent enseignement.
Dans cette pensГ©e cet homme sage, parvenu au comble de la gloire, rГ©solut de consacrer des sanctuaires Г ces deux dieux Еҡiva et AvalokiteЕӣvara.

Or donc lui, ployant dГ©jГ sous le fardeau bГ©ni d'une multitude d'Е“uvres mГ©ritoires, de concert avec son fils et en vue de sa gloire, consacra un Devaliб№…geЕӣvara en l'annГ©e Еӣaka 830 ambara-dahana-maб№…gala, un lundi, le cinquiГЁme jour de la quinzaine claire du mois de jyaiб№Јб№ӯha : le Soleil, le NЕ“ud descendant et Mercure Г©tant dans les GГ©meaux, le Lion Г l'horizon, la Lune entiГЁre dans le Lion, Mars dans la Vierge, le NЕ“ud ascendant dans le Capricorne, Jupiter et Saturne dans le BГ©lier, sous le signe magha.
Enfin, en Еӣaka 833 tri-tri-aб№Јб№ӯa, il consacra, de concert avec son fils aГ®nГ©, dans le village de Cikir, un monastГЁre bouddhique qui fut appelГ© ЕҡrД«-VrМҘddhalokeЕӣvara.
Les points d'appui que la terre instable a trouvГ©s dans les montagnes qui l'affermissent, ce pays les trouvera, grГўce Г Еҡiva et AvalokiteЕӣvara, dans leurs deux sanctuaires.

Voici le total des terrains appartenant au temple de Devaliб№…geЕӣvara : La riziГЁre Bhauk PurДҒk, d'une superficie de 37 galauk et produisant 10 mesures de riz ; elle va de la "maison des hommes de l'enclos des arГ©quiers" jusqu'Г la Grande Mare au Sud. - La rive sud de la mare : 60 galauk, produisant 30 mesures de riz. - Le terrain qui va de la borne du temple sД«ma aux deux rives de la Grande Mare, oГ№ il est bornГ© par le champ Krain et le champ Talavvar : 310 galauk, produisant 80 mesures de riz.

Voici les terres sГЁches oГ№ on sГЁme Г demeure: Le terrain qui va de la borne du temple jusqu'Г la maison du chef des fleuristes ; 73 galauk ; le terrain qui va de la Grande Mara jusqu'Г la "maison du dieu" : 162 galauk. Est donnГ© au temple le terrain appelГ© VrajДҒб№… tout entier ; il part de derriГЁre la forГӘt de la "maison du dieu" pour aller jusqu'Г la forГӘt appelГ©e VandДҒб№…, de la forГӘt VandДҒб№… jusqu'Г la forГӘt des pandanus ; du pied de la forГӘt de la mare du tigre, la forГӘt de l'Г®le de la mare du tigre, le chemin septentrional.

Voici les mares : La mare du tigre, la mare du cerf, la mare du python, la mare AjauГұ CuraiбёҘ, la mare aux briques, la mare KmДҒn, la mare TuГұit, la Grand Mare : celle-ci va de la mare du tigre jusqu'au Nord de la citadelle du seigneur de Trivikramapura, et du Sud de la citadelle de Trivikramapura jusqu'Г ........, tout cela appartient Г ce temple.
V a. Huber prints api ca as though it were not part of the stanza, which he consequently misidentifies as UpagД«ti, whereas it is a regular ДҖryДҒ.
sudaб№ҮбёҚa[vДҒ]sa-: or read sudaб№ҮбёҚavДҒsabhaб№ӯa-? There seems to be space for more lost akб№Јaras than HuberвҖҷs reading suggests. Cf. C. 106, st. III.

XII d. Huber rightly observes in a note that the pДҒda, as written, is deficient by one syllable at the beginnining. This syllable would have had to be long. Options that Yuko Yokochi has proposed to me are tat- (referring to the king), or else syДҒt or sДҒ.
Huber identifies this passages as an ДҖryДҒ stanza (nr. XIV), but this cannot be correct. We believe the passage is prose.
First edited with translation into French by E. Huber (299-311). The inscription is re-edited here by Arlo Griffiths based on autopsy and the EFEO estampages n. 156.
